turns.user_message: I tried taking a long walk this morning just to clear my head, but it completely backfired. Instead of feeling refreshed, I just kept replaying every little mistake I made yesterday. I told myself I would just enjoy the fresh air for twenty minutes, but my brain refused to cooperate. Now I feel even heavier than before I stepped outside, which is honestly ridiculous. This weird slump only started a few days ago, but it already feels exhausting.

Every time I try to fix my mood with a simple routine, I end up feeling worse about myself. I keep telling myself to just push through these recent bad days, but the guilt keeps piling up. I look back at how easily I used to bounce back from minor setbacks, and now I just feel broken. It is frustrating to watch myself struggle with things that used to be so automatic. I keep wondering why I cannot just get my head straight like I used to.

I have reached a point where even the smallest effort to cheer myself up feels like a complete failure. I keep replaying how quickly everything has spiraled out of control since this started, and the shame is absolutely suffocating. I try to act like I am handling it, but deep down I know I am just pretending to be okay. It feels like I have completely lost my grip on my own mind, and I hate that I cannot just snap out of it. Every failed attempt just proves that I am completely ruining my own peace of mind.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This just started recently.
